nameMeanings.name: Walwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Wal(d)wine, which is of ancient Germanic origin, from elements meaning ‘power’ + ‘friend’.

from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Walweyn, Walwain. The central French form of this name is Gawain (see Gavin ). This name is found in the medieval Arthurian romances and is traditionally explained as an adaptation of the Welsh name Gwalchmei, perhaps meaning ‘hawk of the plain’.

variants.name: Gavin
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Gábháin or Ó Gáibhín, both of which are derived from diminutives of gábhadh ‘want’ or ‘danger’ (the second being the more likely meaning here).

Scottish and northern English: from the personal name Gavin, Scottish form of English Gawain and French Gauvain . The name was introduced into English from French versions of the Arthurian romances, where this name was borne by one of the knights of the Round Table, brother of Galahad and Mordred and a nephew of Arthur. It has been taken to be an adaptation of the Welsh name Gwalchmei, perhaps ‘hawk of the plain’.
Scottish: shortened form of McGavin , which is believed to be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gobhann ‘son of the smith’.

nameMeanings.name: Unk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Irish Úna . In Irish legend Úna is the mother of the hero Conn Cétchathach (Conn of the Hundred Battles). It was also the name of the beloved of the 17th-century poet Tomás Láidir Costello : banned by her parents from seeing him, Úna fell into a decline and died, leaving him to mourn her in his verse. The Anglicized form of the name is sometimes taken to be from the feminine of Latin unus ‘one’. It is the name used by Spenser for the lady of the Red Cross Knight in The Faerie Queene: he probably had Latin rather than Irish in mind, even though he worked in Ireland for a while. The Irish name has also been Anglicized as Unity , Juno , Winifred , and Agnes .

variants.name: Juno
meanings: Anglicized form of the Irish name Úna , assimilated to the name of the Roman goddess Juno, consort of Jupiter.

en.lifeSummary: Unk Walwyn had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Unk Walwyn a eu au moins 1 fils.

it.lifeSummary: Unk Walwyn lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Unk Walwynは少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Unk Walwyn(은)는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Unk Walwyn teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: У Unk Walwyn был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Unk Walwyn tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
